revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[arz:ضريح هيمورو]][[en:Himuro Shrine]][[ja:氷室神社]][[zh:冰室神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = {{Nihongo|'''Himuro Shrine'''|氷室神社}} | image = File:Himurojinjya-honden.jpg | caption = The [[torii]] of Himuro Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.6844|N|135.838|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Himuro Gongen|qid=Q134844607|1=Himuro Gongen}}; {{ill|Nintoku|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}; {{ill|Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|qid=Q55177820|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko}}; {{ill|Q55177952|qid=Q55177952|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style ={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} }} '''Himuro Shrine''' (氷室神社, ''Himuro Jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara, Nara Prefecture|1=Nara (city)}}, Japan. It was established in 710. Kami enshrined here include Tsugenoinagi oyamanushi no mikoto, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} and Nukata no Onakatsuhiko no Mikoto (額田大仲彦命). The shrine's main festival is held annually on October 1. Tsugenoinagi oyamanushi no mikoto was a folk figure said to have invited a way to preserve ice. Prince Nukata no Onakatsuhiko no Mikoto, a brother of Emperor Nitoku, met with Tsugenoinagi oyamanushi no mikoto and brought the method to preserve ice to the Emperor ==Overview== {{otheruses|{{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}} located in {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}|Other Himuro Shrines|Himuro Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Infobox religious building| |Name = Himuro Shrine |image = [[File:Himuro-jinja1.jpg|250px]]
Entrance to the precincts {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|41|4|N|135|50|17|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}} Kasuganomachi 1-4 |Latitude = 34|Minutes = 41|Seconds = 4 |Longitude = 135|Minutes = 50|Seconds = 17 |Deities = {{ill|Fighting Rooster Great Mountain Deity|de|Kampfhahn Großberg-Gottheit|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|zh|斗鸡大山主命|lt=Fighting Rooster Inao Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja=闘鶏稲置大山主命|lt_de=Kampfhahn Inao Großberg-Gottheit|lt_zh=斗鸡稻置大山主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fighting Rooster Great Mountain Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Great Parrot Deity|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|Prince Nukata no Oonakahiko|de|Prinz Nukata no Oonakahiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|zh|额田大仲彦皇子|lt=Prince Nukata no Oonakahiko|lt_ja=額田大仲彦命|lt_de=Prinz Nukata no Oonakahiko|lt_zh=额田大仲彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Oonakahiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55177820}} |Status = Shikinai small shrine (theoretical), former {{ill|Village Shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |Foundation = In {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|qid=Q1373026|lt=Wadō|1=Wadō (era)}} 3 ({{ill|710|en|710|qid=Q23518|lt=710 AD|1=710}}) |Main Sanctuary = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Three-Bay Shrine Style Construction|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate Name = |Said Treasure etc = |Annual Festival = {{ill|October 1|en|October 1|qid=Q2913|lt=October 1|1=October 1}} |Sacred Ritual = Ice Donation Festival }} [[File:Weeping cherry tree in Himuro jinja 20150329.jpg|thumb|250px|Weeping Cherry Tree at Himuro Shrine]] '''Himuro Shrine''' (Himuro Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinai Shrine|de|Schrein im Ritualrang|ja|延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|zh|仪式内神社|lt=Small Shrine within the Ritual Ranking|lt_ja=式内小社|lt_de=Kleiner Schrein im Ritualrang|lt_zh=仪式内小神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai Shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11064932}} (theoretical Ranking “Discourse Shrine”) and its former status was that of a {{ill|Village Shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher=Meicho Publishing|year=1989|page=18|isbn=462601335X}}. It once served as a base for the {{ill|South Capital Music Office|de|Dreiseitige Musikbehörde|ja|三方楽所|zh|三方楽所|lt=South Capital Music Office|lt_ja=南都楽所|lt_de=Südhauptstadt-Musikamt|lt_zh=南都乐所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South Capital Music Office|1=South Capital Music Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11355493}}. == History == The shrine’s origin is recorded in the "Himuro Shrine Origin" Emaki, and is also mentioned in the 『{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|qid=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}』 and in the Gen'yōki. {{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|page=19|isbn=4642015493}} On July 22 in Wadō 3 ({{ill|710|en|710|qid=Q23518|lt=710 AD|1=710}}), by the {{ill|Imperial Edict|de|Kaiserlicher Erlass|ja|勅命|zh|敕命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Edict|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅|1=Command paper|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q905765}} of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|qid=Q233224|lt=Emperor Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}}, the shrine was founded by enshrining the ice god at {{ill|Tsukihi Rock|de|Tsukihi-Felsen|ja|月日磐|zh|月日磐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukihi Rock|1=Tsukihi-iwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24865453}} on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|de|Kasuga-Berg|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|zh|春日山|lt=Kasuga Mountain (Nara Prefecture)|lt_ja=春日山 (奈良県)|lt_de=Kasuga-Berg (Nara Präfektur)|lt_zh=春日山 (奈良县)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11513602}}, an area upstream of the Yoshiki River. (In a note, the former shrine location, referred to as Shimotsu Iwanesha, is equated with the Takahashi Shrine in the district of {{ill|Soekami District, Nara|en|Soekami District, Nara|qid=Q7553491|lt=Tenzō District|1=Soekami District, Nara}}, as seen in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Divine Name Register|de|Engi Shiki Divine Name Register|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|zh|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Divine Name Register|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.) In the area known as '''Ice Pond''' in Kasuga – presently part of the {{ill|Nara Kasugano International Forum Iraka|en|Nara Kasugano International Forum Iraka|qid=Q11445169|lt=Nara Kasugano International Forum 甍〜I・RA・KA〜|1=Nara Kasugano International Forum Iraka}} zone – ice was formed by severe cold and stored in the {{ill|Ice house (building)|en|Ice house (building)|qid=Q253893|lt=Himuro|1=Ice house (building)}} (situated in the Asakayahara area overlooking Arakuchi and Sagi ponds, and also known as Kasuga Himuro, Heijō Himuro, or Mikasa Himuro)This was the “Heijō Seven Dynasties Ice Chamber,” also called “Yoshiki River Ice Chamber,” “Kasuga Ice Chamber,” “Heijō Ice Chamber,” “Mikasa Ice Chamber,” “Mizutani Ice Chamber” (per Nara City History and the official website). and the following year, ice was donated to {{ill|Heijō-kyō|en|Heijō-kyō|qid=Q862509|lt=Heijō-kyō|1=Heijō-kyō}}, thus establishing the donation system. On June 1 in Wadō 4 ({{ill|AD 711|en|AD 711|qid=Q23519|lt=711|1=AD 711}}), the first ice-donation ceremony was held; thereafter, from April 1 to September 30 each year, ice was offered at Heijō-kyō. The system was maintained for over 70 years during the 7 generations of the Nara dynasty, but after the capital was relocated to {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|qid=Q751907|lt=Heian|1=Heian-kyō}}, it was abolished. In Jōgan 2 ({{ill|860|en|860|qid=Q24714|lt=860|1=860}}) on February 1, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|qid=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa|1=Emperor Seiwa}}, the shrine was transferred to its present location. There, alongside the deity who taught the method of ice production – {{ill|Fighting Rooster Great Mountain Deity|de|Kampfhahn Großberg-Gottheit|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|zh|斗鸡大山主命|lt=Fighting Rooster Inao Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja=闘鶏稲置大山主命|lt_de=Kampfhahn Inao Großberg-Gottheit|lt_zh=斗鸡稻置大山主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fighting Rooster Great Mountain Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} – two additional deities were enshrined: the one who first presented ice to the emperor, {{ill|Prince Nukata no Oonakahiko|de|Prinz Nukata no Oonakahiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|zh|额田大仲彦皇子|lt=Prince Nukata no Oonakahiko|lt_ja=額田大仲彦命|lt_de=Prinz Nukata no Oonakahiko|lt_zh=额田大仲彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Oonakahiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55177820}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Great Parrot Deity|1=Emperor Nintoku}} – forming a triad. The shrine building is said to have been established in Kenpō 5 ({{ill|1217|en|1217|qid=Q5379|lt=1217|1=1217}}). In the 13th century, one of Japan’s three music offices – the {{ill|South Capital Music Office|de|Dreiseitige Musikbehörde|ja|三方楽所|zh|三方楽所|lt=South Capital Music Office|lt_ja=南都楽所|lt_de=Südhauptstadt-Musikamt|lt_zh=南都乐所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South Capital Music Office|1=South Capital Music Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11355493}} – was set up, transforming Nara into a major center for gagaku (court music), with musicians also serving as priests. As a branch of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|qid=Q714559|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, the annual expenses for festivals, repairs, and maintenance were partially funded by Kasuga Taisha; additional support came from high-end stamped land holdings of {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|qid=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji|1=Kōfuku-ji}} (amounting to 20,000 koku), as well as from stipends for the shrine office and fees of 2,000 koku for the {{ill|South Capital Music Office|de|Dreiseitige Musikbehörde|ja|三方楽所|zh|三方楽所|lt=South Capital Music Office|lt_ja=南都楽所|lt_de=Südhauptstadt-Musikamt|lt_zh=南都乐所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South Capital Music Office|1=South Capital Music Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11355493}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era, following the separation of Shinto and Buddhism ({{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}), the relationship with Kōfuku-ji was severed. In {{ill|1870|en|1870|qid=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), the South Capital Music Office was abolished; it merged with the other two music offices to form the gagaku division of the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|qid=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}}, now known as the {{ill|Board of Ceremonies|en|Board of Ceremonies|qid=Q98687444|lt=Imperial Household Agency Ceremonial Department|1=Board of Ceremonies}}. Furthermore, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Village Shrine|de|Dorfschrein|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, and under the modern shrine Ranking system it is designated as an {{ill|Offering Contribution Shrine|de|Opferbeitrags-Schrein|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|zh|近代社格制度 神饌幣帛料供进社|lt=Offering Contribution Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進社|lt_de=Opferbeitrags-Schrein|lt_zh=供献神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Offering Contribution Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Currently, the shrine is maintained through the support of its {{ill|Shrine Parishioners|de|Schreinfamilie|ja|氏子|zh|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Parishioners|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ujigami|qid=Q767655}} and sponsorship from the frozen ice industry. == Deities == * Principal Deities – {{ill|Fighting Rooster Great Mountain Deity|de|Kampfhahn Großberg-Gottheit|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|zh|斗鸡大山主命|lt=Fighting Rooster Inao Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja=闘鶏稲置大山主命|lt_de=Kampfhahn Inao Großberg-Gottheit|lt_zh=斗鸡稻置大山主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fighting Rooster Great Mountain Deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Great Parrot Deity|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, and {{ill|Prince Nukata no Oonakahiko|de|Prinz Nukata no Oonakahiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|zh|额田大仲彦皇子|lt=Prince Nukata no Oonakahiko|lt_ja=額田大仲彦命|lt_de=Prinz Nukata no Oonakahiko|lt_zh=额田大仲彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Oonakahiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55177820}}. == Precincts == The Kasuga-style first torii faces {{ill|Tōdōji|de|Tōdōji|ja|登大路|zh|登大路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōdōji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大宮通り|1=oomiyadoori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11434469}}. Entering the torii, stone lanterns are lined up on both sides, with a {{ill|Chōzuya|de|Chōzuya|ja|手水舎|zh|手水舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōzuya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 手水|1=Chōzuya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1069667}} to the right and the precinct shrine {{ill|Harado Shrine|de|Harado-Schrein|ja|祓戸社|zh|祓户社|lt=Purification Door Shrine|lt_ja=祓戸社|lt_de=Reinigungstür-Schrein|lt_zh=祓户神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harado Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the left.According to the official precinct map Behind the right-hand lantern and the Chōzuya, a mirror pond and weeping cherry trees are found. Ascending the front stone steps, one encounters the main gate (a four-legged gate) and east–west corridors. [[File:Himurojinjya-honden.jpg|thumb|Main Sanctuary]] * Main Sanctuary (Designated Tangible Cultural Property by Nara Prefecture) – Rebuilt during the {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|qid=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} era ({{ill|1861|en|1861|qid=Q7693|lt=1861|1=1861}} – {{ill|1864|en|1864|qid=Q7704|lt=1864|1=1864}}). It is built in the Ryūzō style with cypress bark roofing. Under the sanctuary’s floor lie two chambers equipped with double-leafed sliding doors. * Worship Hall (Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Nara City) – Also serving as the dance hall, it is a gagaku venue with a balustraded stage. It once formed the central part of the {{ill|South Capital Music Office|de|Dreiseitige Musikbehörde|ja|三方楽所|zh|三方楽所|lt=South Capital Music Office|lt_ja=南都楽所|lt_de=Südhauptstadt-Musikamt|lt_zh=南都乐所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South Capital Music Office|1=South Capital Music Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11355493}}. * Maikōsha – Deity: Koma Hikari-taka. Regarded as the progenitor of the music tradition at the South Capital Music Office and the guardian deity of the musicians. The building is in the single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga Construction|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style and features a display shelf. * Direct Communion Hall – Serving as the administrative and assembly hall. * Guardian Forest * Treasure Repository * Offering Hall * Main Gate (Designated Tangible Cultural Property by Nara Prefecture) – Originally, this gate was the Nichikamon of the {{ill|Imperial Palace|de|Kaiserpalast|ja|内裏|zh|内里|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Palace|1=Dairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3012148}}; it was relocated here in Ōei 9 ({{ill|1402|en|1402|qid=Q6113|lt=1402|1=1402}}). In Kan'ei 18 ({{ill|1641|en|1641|qid=Q6907|lt=1641|1=1641}}), the doors of the Imperial Palace’s Nichikamon and five gold coins were granted. The gate is of the {{ill|Four-legged Gate|de|Vierbeinigtor|ja|四脚門|zh|四脚门|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-legged Gate|1=shikyakumon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420098}}, with a tiled roof and east–west corridors attached. * East Corridor (Designated Tangible Cultural Property by Nara Prefecture) – Originally the dwelling for the portable palanquin from the Imperial Palace, it was relocated here in Ōei 9 ({{ill|1402|en|1402|qid=Q6113|lt=1402|1=1402}}). It measures three ken in length and two ken in width. * West Corridor (Designated Tangible Cultural Property by Nara Prefecture) – Also originally from the Imperial Palace’s portable palanquin dwelling, relocated in Ōei 9 ({{ill|1402|en|1402|qid=Q6113|lt=1402|1=1402}}), measuring three ken by two ken. * Spirit Summoning Shrine – Recognized in {{ill|1877|en|1877|qid=Q7773|lt=1877|1=1877}} (Meiji 10) with the ancestral spirit enshrined on September 21. In {{ill|1889|en|1889|qid=Q7831|lt=1889|1=1889}} (Meiji 22), those who fell during the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|qid=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|1=Satsuma Rebellion}} were also enshrined. The shrine building faces south and is constructed of white wood. * Harado Shrine – Also called the Purification Place or Sumiyoshi Shrine, it is enshrined opposite the Chōzuya. * Mirror Pond == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * A wooden gagaku mask “Ryōō” – A painted wooden mask measuring 29.5 cm in length and 24 cm in width, produced during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}. It is on loan to the {{ill|Nara National Museum|en|Nara National Museum|qid=Q147312|lt=Nara National Museum|1=Nara National Museum}}. === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main Sanctuary * Main Gate and East–West Corridors (3 structures), with an attached Main Gate plaque[http://www.pref.nara.jp/somu-so/jourei/reiki_honbun/k401RG00000904.html Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties] (Nara Prefecture website) === Nara City Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Himuro Shrine Dance Hall (Worship Hall) == Sacred Rituals == === Ice Donation Festival === During the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, in the Kasuga area ({{ill|Kasuga (Nara City)|de|Kasuga (Nara Stadt)|ja|春日 (奈良市)|zh|春日|lt=Kasuga (Nara City)|lt_ja=春日野|lt_de=Kasuga (Nara Stadt)|lt_zh=春日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga (Nara City)|1=Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11513508}}), an area known as '''Ice Pond''' was established – at the present site near {{ill|Nara Kasugano International Forum Iraka|en|Nara Kasugano International Forum Iraka|qid=Q11445169|lt=Nara Kasugano International Forum 甍〜I・RA・KA〜|1=Nara Kasugano International Forum Iraka}} and in the Himuro areaPresumed to be the Asakayahara area overlooking Arakuchi and Sagi ponds (according to the official website) – where the ice god was enshrined and a festival held as a welcome to spring, praying for favorable weather and abundant harvests.On the 1st day of the lunar month in June, known at court as the “Ice New Moon,” ceremonies marking the ice’s seasonal periods were held, while among the people, there was a custom of eating cold and frozen mochi as teething food prepared for the gods and buddhas (per the official website). Today, manufacturers and sellers of ice from all over the country participate in the festival to pray for business success, and it is held on May 1 to avoid the busy period of June. Floral ice displays (including flower-shaped ice and sea bream-shaped ice) – three in number – and six icicles are offered before the deities, while at the dance hall, four gagaku pieces are performed. === Annual Festival === In {{ill|Eikyū|en|Eikyū|qid=Q543201|lt=Eternal|1=Eikyū}} 5 ({{ill|1117|en|1117|qid=Q19538|lt=1117|1=1117}}) on September 1 during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Toba|en|Emperor Toba|qid=Q349284|lt=Emperor Toba|1=Emperor Toba}}, it is said a festival for quelling epidemics was initiated.Official Himuro Shrine website{{Cite web|language=Japanese|date=2020-10-01|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20201001-VOOJTCPQU5NJRDOL5GVQDUCYME/|title=Nara – Himuro Shrine "Procession" Revived After 58 Years with Robotic Support|website=Sankei News|publisher=Sankei Shimbun|accessdate=2020-10-11}} Although the custom temporarily ceased following the turmoil of the {{ill|Genpei War|en|Genpei War|qid=Q737542|lt=Genpei Turmoil|1=Genpei War}}, it was revived in Kenpō 5 ({{ill|1217|en|1217|qid=Q5379|lt=1217|1=1217}}) by {{ill|Emperor Juntoku|en|Emperor Juntoku|qid=Q357249|lt=Emperor Juntoku|1=Emperor Juntoku}}, enshrining the guardian deity of the {{ill|South Capital Music Office|de|Dreiseitige Musikbehörde|ja|三方楽所|zh|三方楽所|lt=South Capital Music Office|lt_ja=南都楽所|lt_de=Südhauptstadt-Musikamt|lt_zh=南都乐所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/South Capital Music Office|1=South Capital Music Office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11355493}} – and it is said that even messengers of the sun paid their respects. Until the end of the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the festival – known as the “Himuro Gagaku Festival” – included a procession that started at 18:00 on the first day and continued until 20:00, while the subsequent banquet festival on the next day featured additional gagaku; records state that as many as 38 pieces were performed and gagaku was offered throughout the day. Until the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} period, the procession’s resting place was at the entrance of the former {{ill|Ichijō-in|de|Ichijō-in|ja|一乗院|zh|一乘院|lt=Ichijō-in Palace|lt_ja=一乗院宮邸|lt_de=Ichijō-in-Palast|lt_zh=一乘院宫邸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in|1=Ichijō-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11352379}}, where a {{ill|Phoenix Palanquin|de|Phönix-Sänft|ja|鳳輦|zh|凤辇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Phoenix Palanquin|1=hōren|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675302}} (a {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|qid=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}}) was enshrined, with the head priest from {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|qid=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji|1=Kōfuku-ji}} participating in the festival. It is said that, along the route between the shrine and the resting place, revelers merrily walked in a custom called “Dōraku.” From the Meiji era onward, the site of the former South Gate of Kōfuku-ji became the resting place. Later, at the request of the parishioners, the procession was rerouted to cover 32 districts within the parish; however, due to traffic issues, it was discontinued in {{ill|1962|en|1962|qid=Q2764|lt=1962|1=1962}} (Shōwa 37). In {{ill|2020|en|2020|qid=Q25337|lt=2020|1=2020}} (Reiwa 2), to commemorate the restoration of the Phoenix Palanquin, the procession was revived for the first time in 58 years (between the shrine and the resting place at the former South Gate of Kōfuku-ji). ==== Current Annual Festival ==== Currently, a rotation of 3 to 4 districts is assigned each year, with an evening festival held on September 30 and the main festival on October 1. * Evening Festival – Begins at 17:00 on September 30 * Morning Festival – Begins at 11:00 on October 1 * Evening Festival – Begins at 17:00 on October 1 ==== Ice Illumination ==== Within the shrine precincts, around 18:00 on both the evening and main festival days—and in each parish district from around 18:00 during the evening festival—candles are lit on ema plaques and perforated ice blocks are displayed as “Ice Illumination.” === Sacred Ritual Gallery === File:Himuro Jinja Reisai Torii 01.jpg|Scene of the Evening Festival (Torii) File:Himuro Jinja Reisai KoriKentou.jpg|Ice Illumination File:Himuro Jinja Reisai Sando.jpg|Pathway Decorated with Ice Illumination File:Himuro Jinja Reisai Gagaku.jpg|Gagaku Performance Scene File:Himuro Jinja Reisai Enbu.jpg|Gagaku “Enbu” File:Himuro Jinja Reisai Ranryooh.jpg|Gagaku “Ranryōō” File:Himuro Jinja Reisai Nasori.jpg|Gagaku “Nasori” File:Himuro Jinja Reisai Batou.jpg|Gagaku “Batō” File:Himuro Jinja Reisai Rakuson.jpg|Gagaku “Rakuson” File:Himuro Jinja Reisai Tessen.jpg|Returning Offerings to the Offering Hall == Local Information == ; Location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}} Kasuganomachi 1-4 ; Access * A 15‐minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|qid=Q6414371|lt=Nara Station|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}. * From Nara Station, take the city loop bus operated by {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|en|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|qid=Q11445000|lt=Nara Kōtsū|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} and alight immediately at "Himuro Shrine/{{ill|Nara National Museum|en|Nara National Museum|qid=Q147312|lt=National Museum|1=Nara National Museum}} Front." ; Surroundings * {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|qid=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park|1=Nara Park}} * {{ill|Kōfuku-ji|en|Kōfuku-ji|qid=Q1070863|lt=Kōfuku-ji|1=Kōfuku-ji}} * {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|qid=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}} == Notes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == Sources == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ice house (building)|en|Ice house (building)|qid=Q253893|lt=Himuro|1=Ice house (building)}} * {{ill|Fighting Rooster Country Chief|de|Fighting Rooster Country Chief|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|zh|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fighting Rooster Country Chief|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Qanat|en|Qanat|qid=Q459297|lt=Kanat|1=Qanat}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Himuro-jinja}} {{osm box|n|2618209171}} * [http://www.himurojinja.jp/ Official Himuro Shrine Website] * [http://www.nantogakuso.jp/ Official South Capital Music Office Website] {{pref-stub-Nara Prefecture}} [[shinto-stub]] ==Gallery== Guide to the Sacred Sites of Japan's Ancient Religion'' (University of Hawai'i Press, 2013). ==External links== *[http://www.himurojinja.jp Official website] {{Commonscat|Himuro-jinja}}
